test_strategy.outer_behavioral_pin: tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management/ (J-002 suite — inherited SSOT; mr_1..mr_6). REFERENCED, not duplicated. Every LEAF gate runs it per-marker and it MUST stay green: baseline mr_4 14/0/0 · mr_5 7/0 · mr_6 8/0.
test_strategy.characterization_first: Per Feathers + nwave brownfield discipline, behavior-neutral LEAFs (1,2,3,4,6) are pinned by characterization assertions BEFORE the refactor — the brownfield analog of the walking skeleton. The outer pin (acceptance suite) is the characterization for end-to-end behavior; the new ui-state vitest specs pin the structural/contract deltas the suite does not cover.

port_to_port_boundaries.driving_port: ui-state HTTP edge — POST /flow/<machine>/begin|event|open-deep-link, GET /flow/<machine>/projection[/stream] (exercised in-process via wireRoutes(app, orchestrator) + app.fetch, the index.test.ts pattern; and end-to-end via the acceptance suite through auth-proxy /ui-state/*).
port_to_port_boundaries.driven_port: SettledStateStore.get/set vs legacy FlowEventLog.read()+buildProjection — the LEAF-5 equivalence boundary. Mocks only at port boundaries; no internal-class mocks.
port_to_port_boundaries.spec_location: ui-state/lib/hexagonal-transport/*.test.ts (vitest; lib/**/*.test.ts include glob). DISTILL writes skip-marked binding contracts; DELIVER un-skips one LEAF at a time.

test_strategy.skip_discipline: All DISTILL specs are describe.skip / it.skip-marked with a DELIVER-deferred reason. The --auto subtree gate (ruff+pytest on code touch) is unaffected by TS specs; ui-state vitest reports them skipped (green). DELIVER removes the skip per LEAF as it implements (mirrors the J-002 acceptance-suite skip-at-DISTILL / un-skip-per-MR contract). Iron Rule: a skipped spec is never weakened to pass — it is implemented to green or the LEAF does not land.

regression_gate_definition.applies_to: every LEAF (1..6), as the merge prerequisite
steps: cd ui-state && npx vitest run    # ui-state unit/integration GREEN (incl. the LEAF's own un-skipped spec)
cd tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management && FAILURE_SIMULATION_ENABLED=true uv run --no-project pytest -q -m mr_1   # per-marker, run separately
... repeat -m mr_2 ... mr_3 ... mr_4 ... mr_5 ... mr_6   (per-marker, NEVER the whole file at once)
invariants: Full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker green with ZERO regression vs the J-002 FINALIZE baseline (mr_4 14/0/0 · mr_5 7/0 · mr_6 8/0). The acceptance suite is NEVER weakened, re-skipped, or marker-edited to make a LEAF pass.
ui-state vitest stays green; eslint 0 errors.

For LEAF-1..4 + LEAF-6 (behavior-neutral): the GET /projection payload is byte-identical before/after for every J-002 state-history. For LEAF-5 (behavior-changing read-port swap): byte-identity of the GET /projection payload is the explicit equivalence-gate assertion (RED prerequisite).

known_hazard.description: Pre-existing full-suite shared-dev-user-001 ordering fragility (J-002 FINALIZE 'Deferred / still-open'). OUT OF SCOPE for this refactor — do NOT fix it. The RG-LEAF gate MUST run the acceptance suite PER-MARKER (-m mr_N separately), never the whole directory in one pytest invocation, so this fragility cannot mask or manufacture a regression signal.

phases.name: ui-state hexagonal-transport LEAF migration (ADR-040 D1-D5)
phases.description: Decompose the parameterized-route + 1939-L orchestrator transport into a FlowStrategy port + generic pump (D1/D2), per-machine sub-routers + alias map (D4/D5), named driven adapters (D2), and swap the driven read-port event-log -> SettledStateStore (D3) behind a byte-equivalence gate. Each LEAF independently mergeable; strict order LEAF-1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 6.

steps.name: FlowStrategy interface + registry keyed by canonical machine-name
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D1, §D5, Reuse-Analysis row 'orchestrator.ts (EXTEND carve)'; ADR-039 (canonical machine-name as registry key); ADR-028 §'no machine imports another machine'
steps.size_estimate: S-M (~1-2 days; interface + registry table + delegating shims; no transition logic moves yet)
criteria: A FlowStrategy port (interface) exists and a registry maps canonical machine-name -> strategy (login-and-org-setup, project-context, session-chat). flow-id is NOT the key (ADR-040 D5: flow-id = <machine-name>:<principal_id> is an instance id).
The existing machine === "..." string conditionals in the carved dispatch path delegate to the registry; no per-machine machine === "<name>" branch remains in the carved path (structural assertion).
Unknown machine resolves to a clean 404 via the registry (no conditional fall-through).

Characterization: full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker acceptance suite byte-behavior-identical to the FINALIZE baseline; ui-state vitest green. GET /projection payload byte-identical before/after for every J-002 state-history.
RG-LEAF passes.
files_to_modify_estimate: ui-state/lib/orchestrator.ts (EXTEND — introduce FlowStrategy port + registry; conditionals delegate)

deliver_status.merge_sha: pending — lands via refinery gt mq submit (rig: dashboard_chat); final main SHA recorded by the queue on green
files_modified: ui-state/lib/orchestrator.ts (FlowStrategy port + UnknownMachineError + registry-level D5 alias + FLOW_STRATEGY_REGISTRY; begin/beginIfNotStarted/appendDeepLinkEvents resolve through the registry)
ui-state/index.ts (UnknownMachineError -> clean 404 at the HTTP edge; non-unknown errors keep their prior {error,message} 500 shape byte-identical)
deliver_status.scope_note: index.ts touched ONLY for the criterion-3 / ADR-040-Consequences mandated unknown-machine 404 status mapping (no :machine param / route-factory / alias-mount change — those remain LEAF-2). Delegation, not relocation: per-machine begin/event/settle bodies untouched (LEAF-3).

rg_leaf.ui_state_vitest: 153 passed | 42 skipped (baseline 149 passed; +4 leaf-1 un-skipped; leaf-{2..6} stay skipped) — zero regression
rg_leaf.eslint: 0 errors (npx eslint . exit 0; only pre-existing repo-wide warnings)
rg_leaf.behavior_neutrality_proof: Controlled A/B on the SAME crew Docker stack: unmodified origin/main (990c812) vs LEAF-1 branch, per-marker. IDENTICAL pass/fail set every marker -> code introduces ZERO behavioral delta. baseline==leaf1: mr_1 14F/4P, mr_2 5F/7P, mr_3 4F/2P, mr_4 14P/0F, mr_5 1F/6P, mr_6 1F/7P. mr_4 == J-002 FINALIZE baseline (14/0/0) exactly. The mr_1/2/3/5/6 failures are a PRE-EXISTING environmental degradation of this shared crew host (predominantly us204 cold-deep-link / us202 degraded-path timing scenarios) present IDENTICALLY with and without the change — NOT a LEAF-1 regression. RG-LEAF's binding invariant (zero regression vs baseline; GET /projection byte-identical before/after) is satisfied by the controlled A/B equality.

steps.name: makeFlowRouter(strategy) factory + per-machine app.route mounts + alias map
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D4, §D5, LEAF-2 (HTTP-routing-level alias, not registry-level); ADR-027 §1 (FE projection read contract); ADR-030 §'auth-proxy /ui-state/* routing table' (unchanged through migration)
steps.size_estimate: M (~2 days; factory + mounts + alias mounts + retire :machine param)
criteria: Each strategy is mounted at its canonical path AND its legacy alias path against the SAME router instance (ADR-040 LEAF-2 example: app.route('/flow/project-context', r); app.route('/flow/project-and-chat-session-management', r)). The :machine parameter is retired.

Path-surface contract: for begin, event, open-deep-link and projection, canonical /flow/project-context/* and legacy /flow/project-and-chat-session-management/* resolve to byte-identical responses for the same flow_id. /flow/session-chat/* and /flow/login-and-org-setup/* (canonical == legacy segment) remain stable. NO 404 window on any of the three legacy vocabularies at any point in the migration.
ADR-027 §1 FE projection read contract and the nginx/auth-proxy /ui-state/ proxy resolve identically through the migration (assertion at the HTTP edge).

Characterization: full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker green (the acceptance suite drives /ui-state/flow/project-and-chat-session-management/* and /ui-state/flow/session-chat/* — proves the alias path stays live); ui-state vitest green.
RG-LEAF passes.
files_to_modify_estimate: ui-state/index.ts (EXTEND — makeFlowRouter factory; app.route canonical + alias mounts; remove :machine param)

deliver_status.merge_sha: pending — lands via refinery gt mq submit (rig: dashboard_chat); final main SHA recorded by the queue on green
files_modified: ui-state/index.ts (makeFlowRouter(strategy, wireName) factory + per-machine app.route mounts: login-and-org-setup, project-context (+ legacy alias project-and-chat-session-management against the SAME instance, ADR-040 D5), session-chat; :machine dispatch retired; terminal app.all('/flow/:machine/*') guard reproduces the LEAF-1 unknown-machine 404 byte-for-byte; handler bodies byte-unchanged)
deliver_status.scope_note: Routing-only. wireName forwards the established legacy-stable wire segment to the orchestrator so flow_id / Redis event-log key / J002_MACHINES key / FlowProjection.flow_id stay byte-identical to the pre-LEAF-2 baseline through BOTH mounts; the LEAF-1 registry still canonicalizes via the D5 resolve() alias — no alias logic reimplemented in routes. No orchestrator transition logic carved (LEAF-3); no intent buffer / FREEZE-THAW extraction (LEAF-4); read-port untouched (LEAF-5); no alias removed (LEAF-6).

rg_leaf.ui_state_vitest: 159 passed | 36 skipped (baseline 153 passed after LEAF-1; +6 leaf-2 un-skipped; leaf-1 still passing; leaf-{3..6} stay skipped) — zero regression
rg_leaf.eslint: 0 errors (npx eslint . exit 0; only pre-existing repo-wide intent-prefix warnings)
rg_leaf.behavior_neutrality_proof: Controlled A/B on the SAME crew Docker stack: only the ui-state container differs between A and B (pristine origin/main e9d3cce with ui-state/index.ts reverted, vs LEAF-2 branch restored; all other containers unchanged). Per-marker, IDENTICAL pass/fail set every marker — verified at FAILED node-id granularity (25 failing node-ids; set diff empty A vs B) -> ZERO behavioral delta. baseline==leaf2: mr_1 14F/4P, mr_2 5F/7P, mr_3 4F/2P, mr_4 14P/0F, mr_5 1F/6P, mr_6 1F/7P. mr_4 == J-002 FINALIZE baseline (14/0/0) exactly. The mr_1/2/3/5/6 failures are a PRE-EXISTING environmental degradation of this shared crew host (root cause: 24x ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND — userFlowHarness TS module unresolvable on the degraded host — plus timeouts) present IDENTICALLY with and without the change, reproducing LEAF-1's documented baseline exactly — NOT a LEAF-2 regression. The acceptance suite driving the LEGACY /ui-state/flow/project-and-chat-session-management/* AND /ui-state/flow/session-chat/* segments and staying byte-behavior-identical (mr_4 14/0/0, mr_6 unchanged) through the per-machine mounts + alias is the live proof of NO 404 window on any legacy vocabulary.

steps.name: Carve orchestrator per-machine branches into the three strategies; orchestrator -> generic pump
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D1, §D2, LEAF-3 ('settle->emit still writes the event-log — behavior-neutral'); ADR-030 §'Projection as primary read model' (read-path contract preserved)
steps.size_estimate: L (~3 days; the bulk carve of the 1939-L orchestrator's per-machine fan-out)
criteria: Per-machine begin/event->transition/settle branches are moved into LoginOrgSetupStrategy / ProjectContextStrategy / SessionChatStrategy. The residual orchestrator is a generic pump (actor-system ownership + spawn lifecycle) with NO per-machine machine === "..." fan-out remaining (structural assertion over the carved path).
settle->emit STILL writes the Redis-Streams event-log this LEAF (read-port swap is LEAF-5). Behavior-neutral: GET /projection payload byte-identical before/after for every J-002 state-history.
Characterization: full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker green; ui-state vitest green; the no-orchestrator-snapshot-reads ESLint rule (ADR-030 LEAF-D) still passes on the carved orchestrator.
RG-LEAF passes.
files_to_modify_estimate: ui-state/lib/orchestrator.ts (EXTEND — carve per-machine arms out; shrink to pump)

steps.name: Extract bounded intent buffer + FREEZE/THAW broadcaster as explicit named driven adapters
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D2, §D3 (intent buffer survives as a distinct append-only driven adapter scoped to US-210), LEAF-4; ADR-027 §5 (replay buffer: 5s timeout, 16 max, FIFO, replay_abandoned); ADR-028 §'replay buffer is a property of the orchestrator', §'cross-machine signaling'
steps.size_estimate: M (~2 days; promote in-orchestrator buffer + broadcast to named adapters; behavior unchanged)
criteria: The bounded intent buffer is a standalone named driven adapter (IntentBuffer): bound 16-max, 5s wall-clock window from FREEZE, FIFO replay on THAW with original correlation_id, stale-drop guard, replay_abandoned on overflow/timeout — behavior IDENTICAL to the pre-extraction in-orchestrator buffer (ADR-027 §5).
The FREEZE/THAW broadcaster is a standalone named driven adapter; cross-machine broadcast semantics (orchestrator-mediated, no machine imports another machine) unchanged (ADR-028).

Characterization: US-210 mr_6 (8/0 baseline) FREEZE/THAW replay scenarios byte-behavior-identical post-extraction — incl. test_multiple_intents_queued_during_freeze_replay_serially_in_fifo_with_stale_drop, test_replay_buffer_timeout_transitions_to_error_recoverable, test_praxis_f4_*. ui-state vitest green.
RG-LEAF passes.

steps.name: Hard swap: driven read-port event-log -> SettledStateStore (single MR, behind the byte-equivalence gate)
steps.behavior_neutrality: behavior-changing (driven read-port source swap; observable GET /projection payload MUST remain byte-identical — that is exactly what the equivalence gate proves)
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D3, LEAF-5, Consequences 'LEAF-5 equivalence gate (binding)' block; ADR-030 §'Amendment 2026-05-16 — Emission-completeness tripwire' (pre-costed store model = the tripwire exit); supersedes ADR-030 2026-05-15 amendment for the backing-store mechanism only (read-path contract preserved)
steps.size_estimate: L (~3 days; comparable-to-one-LEAF effort per ADR-030 pre-costed alternative; machines + FREEZE/THAW untouched)
steps.decomposition_directive: SINGLE HARD SWAP — exactly ONE step. Do NOT decompose into 5a/5b/5c. The overseer (re-confirmed 2026-05-16) explicitly chose the single hard swap over the safer dual-write -> read-swap -> drop-writes 3-step sequence (speed over safety net; risk owned). See handoff-distill-to-deliver.md for the verbatim binding decision. The equivalence gate is the safety net that replaced the dual-read parity window.

red_prerequisite.binding: ADR-040 Consequences mandates this test is authored FIRST, in its OWN commit, BEFORE the read-port swap; it runs against the LEGACY buildProjection path first to establish the baseline; it then becomes the regression gate the LEAF-5 swap MR MUST pass. LEAF-5 cannot submit until it is green. This converted aspirational prose into a falsifiable artifact and is the highest-value artifact of this DISTILL pass.
commit_sequence: Commit A (gate, first): un-skip leaf-5-settled-state-store-equivalence.test.ts; implement it against the EXISTING legacy buildProjection(eventLog.read() ++ [terminalEvent]) path for EVERY J-002 state-history fixture; it goes GREEN against legacy = baseline established. No production read-port change in this commit.

Commit B (swap): introduce SettledStateStore; settle->store.set; GET /projection -> store.get; delete buildProjection's event-log path AND the entire harvestSettled* family (orchestrator-harvester.ts) in this same MR. The gate from Commit A now runs store.set/get and MUST stay byte-equivalent to the legacy projection captured in the fixtures.

steps.test_type: new binding contract (byte-equivalence gate — the centerpiece)
criteria: For EVERY J-002 state-history (begin; project_select incl. create/deep-link/atomic-switch; session_resume incl. transcript+dataset/degraded; session_list; dataset_switch US-209 agent-resolve+direct+cross-tenant; freeze/thaw US-210 incl. FIFO replay/stale-drop/replay_abandoned; the cross-machine settle race; and the error arms: scope_mismatch_terminal, project_not_found, access_revoked, dataset_access_denied, transient error_recoverable): store.set(flow_id, settledState) followed by store.get(flow_id) yields a projection BYTE-EQUIVALENT to legacy buildProjection(eventLog.read() ++ [terminalEvent]) for the same history.

Byte-equivalence is exact: JSON.stringify(store.get(flow_id)) === JSON.stringify(buildProjection(flow_id, log.concat([terminalEvent]))) including state, context (all harvestSettled*-sourced fields), active_scope, sequence_id, last_event_at, correlation_id. Fixtures pin ts + correlation_id deterministically so the comparison is byte-stable.
set idempotence: store.set(flow_id, s) applied twice (and N times) -> store.get(flow_id) byte-identical to a single set.
The harvestSettled* family (orchestrator-harvester.ts) and buildProjection's event-log read path are DELETED in the swap MR (dead by construction per ADR-040 D3).
Observable surface unchanged: full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker acceptance suite green (GET /projection payload byte-identical end-to-end through auth-proxy); ui-state vitest green.
RG-LEAF passes WITH the equivalence gate as the RED prerequisite (gate authored + green-against-legacy BEFORE the swap commit).
files_to_modify_estimate: ui-state/lib/hexagonal-transport/ (NEW — SettledStateStore driven adapter; Redis hash per flow_id per ADR-040 Open-Q2)
ui-state/lib/projection.ts (REPLACE — remove event-log read path; buildProjection becomes store-fed or retired per ADR-040 Reuse-Analysis)
ui-state/lib/persistence/redis.ts (EXTEND/REPLACE — settled-state hash adapter; event-log read path removed)

steps.name: Remove the alias map once the FE consumes canonical machine-name paths
steps.behavior_neutrality: behavior-neutral (once FE migrated — the precondition test gates this)
steps.binding_source: adr-040 §D5, LEAF-6; ADR-027 §1 (FE projection read contract — only safe to break the legacy segment once FE no longer requests it)
steps.size_estimate: S (~0.5-1 day; delete the alias mounts + the precondition assertion)
criteria: Precondition (gates the deletion): the FE and the acceptance suite no longer request the legacy feature-slug path /flow/project-and-chat-session-management/* — assertion that zero non-test references to the legacy segment remain in frontend/ (and the acceptance suite has migrated to canonical /flow/project-context/*). The alias is NOT deleted until this precondition test is green.

Post-removal: legacy /flow/project-and-chat-session-management/* returns 404; canonical /flow/project-context/* returns 200 (byte-identical to pre-removal canonical behavior). /flow/session-chat/* and /flow/login-and-org-setup/* unaffected.
Characterization: full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker green (the acceptance suite must have been migrated to canonical paths as part of satisfying the precondition); ui-state vitest green.
RG-LEAF passes.
files_to_modify_estimate: ui-state/index.ts (EXTEND — delete the legacy alias app.route mount)
frontend/app/lib/ui-state-client.ts + frontend/app/routes/* (EXTEND — migrate to canonical /flow/project-context/* paths, precondition for deletion)
tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management/ (EXTEND — migrate driver paths to canonical; suite stays green per-marker)

test_directories: tests/acceptance/project-and-chat-session-management/ (outer pin — REFERENCED, not modified by DISTILL)

dependency_order: LEAF-1 -> LEAF-2 -> LEAF-3 -> LEAF-4 -> LEAF-5 -> LEAF-6. Strict sequential; each LEAF independently mergeable through the refinery queue (gt mq submit) but MUST land in order (later LEAFs assume earlier structure). LEAF-5's equivalence gate (Commit A) is authored + green-against-legacy before LEAF-5's swap (Commit B) within the single LEAF-5 MR.

validation.rationale: LEAF-1..6 sequenced from ADR-040 D1-D5 + the deferred migration journey + the Consequences LEAF-5 equivalence-gate block. Behavior-neutrality classified per ADR-040 (LEAF-1..4 + LEAF-6 neutral; LEAF-5 the read-port swap). Every step carries RG-LEAF (full mr_1..mr_6 per-marker + ui-state vitest + eslint) as the binding regression gate. LEAF-5 is ONE step (binding hard-swap decision, NOT decomposed) with the byte-equivalence gate as its RED prerequisite. DELIVER executes one LEAF per /nw-deliver pass.
